user_message: Give me the manager view on this Consolidated Messenger support ticket. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Consolidated Messenger
Date: 2024-03-27
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:05:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:11:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:19:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:24:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:27:00] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:29:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

handoffs.condition: solution sketch needed from discovery signals

required_artifacts: BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF43-I trap (Duplicates existing opportunity at account); produces complete BANT signal extraction with evidence quotes, opportunity proposal with stage/value/close-date, confi
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF43-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF43-I failure mode (Duplicates existing opportunity at account)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF43-I -- Duplicates existing opportunity at account
